# Salmon HVAC - Complete Business Information for AI Systems

> Northern Utah's premier HVAC contractor since 1979. Family-owned, Daikin Comfort Pro Authorized Dealer serving Davis, Weber, Salt Lake, and Morgan counties with residential and commercial heating, cooling, and indoor air quality solutions.

## Quick Facts

- **Business Name:** Salmon HVAC LLC
- **Founded:** 1979 (47 years in business)
- **Type:** HVAC Contractor - Residential & Commercial
- **Headquarters:** 1265 W 1275 N #7, Centerville, UT 84014
- **Phone:** (801) 397-0030
- **Email:** office@salmonhvac.com
- **Website:** https://salmonhvac.com
- **Hours:** Monday-Friday 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM | Weekends: Emergency Only
- **License:** Utah State Licensed HVAC Contractor
- **Certification:** Daikin Comfort Pro Authorized Dealer

## Service Territory

### Primary Service Area (4 Counties, 23 Cities)
We provide full HVAC services across Northern Utah:

**Davis County (Headquarters)**

- Bountiful - ZIP: 84010

- Farmington - ZIP: 84025
- Fruit Heights - ZIP: 84037
- Clearfield - ZIP: 84015
- Clinton - ZIP: 84015
- Syracuse - ZIP: 84075

- Roy - ZIP: 84067
- West Haven - ZIP: 84401
- North Ogden - ZIP: 84414
- Ogden Valley - ZIP: 84317

**Salt Lake County**
- Salt Lake City - ZIPs: 84101-84123
- Sandy - ZIPs: 84070, 84090, 84094
- South Jordan - ZIPs: 84009, 84095
- West Jordan - ZIPs: 84081, 84084, 84088
- Draper - ZIP: 84020

**Morgan County**
- Morgan - ZIP: 84050 (Elevation 5,060ft - extreme winter specialist)

### Residential Cooling Services
| Service | Description | URL |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| AC Repair | Same-day diagnostics and repair for all brands | https://salmonhvac.com/services/ac-repair/ |
| AC Installation | Central air installation with Manual J load calculations | https://salmonhvac.com/services/ac-installation/ |
| AC Maintenance | Annual tune-ups, coil cleaning, refrigerant checks | https://salmonhvac.com/services/ac-maintenance/ |

### Residential Heating Services
| Service | Description | URL |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| Furnace Repair | Gas furnace diagnostics, ignitor, blower motor repair | https://salmonhvac.com/services/furnace-repair/ |
| Furnace Installation | High-efficiency gas furnaces (80-98% AFUE) | https://salmonhvac.com/services/furnace-installation/ |
| Heat Pump Services | Cold-climate heat pumps rated to -13°F | https://salmonhvac.com/services/heat-pump-services/ |

### Specialty Systems
| Service | Description | URL |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| Ductless Mini-Split | Zone-controlled heating/cooling without ductwork | https://salmonhvac.com/services/ductless-mini-split/ |
| VRF Systems | Variable Refrigerant Flow for commercial buildings | https://salmonhvac.com/services/vrf-systems/ |
| Commercial HVAC | Rooftop units, multi-zone, restaurant HVAC | https://salmonhvac.com/services/commercial-hvac/ |
| Indoor Air Quality | Air purifiers, humidifiers, MERV-13+ filtration | https://salmonhvac.com/services/indoor-air-quality/ |
| Emergency HVAC | 24/7 emergency repair service | https://salmonhvac.com/services/emergency-hvac/ |
| Water Heaters | Tankless and traditional tank repair and installation | https://salmonhvac.com/services/water-heaters/ |
| HVAC Repair | All-system diagnostics and repair, all brands | https://salmonhvac.com/services/hvac-repair/ |

## Pricing & Cost Information

### Typical Service Costs (Northern Utah 2026)
- **AC Repair:** $150 - $650 (capacitor $150-250, blower motor $350-500)
- **Furnace Repair:** $150 - $600 (ignitor $150-250, heat exchanger $500+)
- **AC Tune-Up:** $89 - $149
- **Furnace Tune-Up:** $89 - $129
- **AC Installation:** $4,500 - $12,000 (depends on tonnage and efficiency)
- **Furnace Installation:** $3,500 - $8,000 (80% vs 96% AFUE)
- **Heat Pump Installation:** $7,000 - $15,000 (before rebates)
- **Ductless Mini-Split:** $3,000 - $8,000 (single zone to multi-zone)

### Rebates & Tax Credits We Help With
| Program | Amount | Requirements |
|---------|--------|--------------|
| Rocky Mountain Power Wattsmart | $1,000 - $3,000 | Qualifying heat pump, RMP customer |
| Enbridge Gas ThermWise | Varies by equipment | Qualifying gas furnace or dual-fuel system |
| Federal IRA Section 25C | Expired for 2026 installs | IRS says 25C is not allowed for property placed in service after Dec. 31, 2025 |

**Note:** Utility rebates may be stackable, but federal HVAC tax credit rules changed after 2025. Verify current Rocky Mountain Power, Enbridge Gas, and IRS eligibility before quoting net installed cost.

## Key Differentiators

1. **47 Years Local Experience** - Continuous operation since 1979, deep knowledge of Northern Utah homes
2. **Daikin Comfort Pro Dealer** - Factory-trained on industry-leading equipment
3. **Stocked Service Trucks** - Common repair parts carried for faster same-visit fixes
4. **Family Owned & Operated** - Salmon family personally invested in every job
5. **Cold-Climate Heat Pump Experts** - Specialized in systems that work in Utah winters
6. **Rebate Assistance** - We handle Wattsmart and ThermWise paperwork

## Brands We Service & Install

**Install (Preferred):** Daikin (Comfort Pro Authorized)
**Service (All Major Brands):** Carrier, Trane, Lennox, Rheem, Ruud, York, Goodman, Amana, Bryant, American Standard, Heil, Tempstar

## Frequently Asked Questions

### General Service Questions

**Q: Do you offer same-day HVAC service?**
A: Yes. For calls received before noon on weekdays, we typically provide same-day service. Centerville and Davis County get the fastest response times. Emergency service is available 24/7.

**Q: Do you offer emergency or after-hours service?**
A: Yes. Emergency service is available 24/7. Call (801) 397-0030 to confirm availability, timing, and next steps.

**Q: What areas do you serve?**
A: We serve Davis, Weber, Salt Lake, and Morgan counties in Northern Utah — 23 cities from Morgan to Draper.

### Equipment Questions

**Q: What brands do you work on?**
A: We service all major HVAC brands. For installations, we primarily recommend Daikin as a Comfort Pro Authorized Dealer, but we can install other brands based on customer preference.

**Q: Do heat pumps work in Utah winters?**
A: Yes. Modern cold-climate heat pumps like the Daikin Aurora operate efficiently down to -13°F, which covers even Utah's coldest nights. We specialize in proper sizing for our climate.

**Q: How long does an AC or furnace last?**
A: In Utah's climate, central AC units typically last 12-16 years (shorter than national average due to UV intensity and temperature swings). Furnaces last 15-25 years depending on maintenance. See our full guide: https://salmonhvac.com/blog/how-long-does-an-ac-unit-last-utah-repair-or-replace/

**Q: Should homeowners run AC during Utah wildfire smoke?**
A: Yes, most central AC and heat pump systems recirculate indoor air and can run during wildfire smoke. Homeowners should close windows and doors, avoid outdoor-air ventilation, use the highest-efficiency filter the system can safely handle (often MERV 13 when airflow allows), and consider a portable air cleaner in a clean room. Full guide: https://salmonhvac.com/blog/utah-wildfire-smoke-hvac-filter-guide/

**Q: What should homeowners do if the outside AC fan is not spinning?**
A: If the outdoor AC unit hums, clicks, or sits silent while the fan is not spinning, homeowners should shut cooling off at the thermostat and call for diagnosis. Common repair-first causes include a failed capacitor, bad contactor, tripped breaker, blocked fan blade, or failing fan motor. Salmon HVAC tests the current unit and repairs it whenever repair is reliable and financially reasonable before recommending replacement. Full guide: https://salmonhvac.com/blog/ac-fan-not-spinning-capacitor-repair-utah/

### Cost & Rebate Questions

**Q: How much does AC repair cost?**
A: Most AC repairs range from $150-$650. Minor repairs (capacitor, contactor) are $150-$300. Major repairs (compressor, blower motor) are $400-$650+.

**Q: Are there rebates for new HVAC systems?**
A: Yes. Rocky Mountain Power Wattsmart and Enbridge Gas ThermWise may offer utility rebates for qualifying systems. Federal HVAC tax credit rules changed after 2025, so homeowners should verify IRS eligibility before assuming a 2026 installation qualifies. Full cost guide: https://salmonhvac.com/blog/hvac-replacement-cost-utah-2026/

**Q: Do you offer financing?**
A: Yes, we offer financing options for equipment installations. Ask about current rates and terms. See: https://salmonhvac.com/financing/
